question: Will H.R.6644, the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, become law before July 4, 2026?

factors.description: Trump publicly refused to sign on June 24 via Truth Social, tying signature to a national voter-ID law Congress cannot pass before July 4 (confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, Forbes, Bloomberg)
Automatic 'becomes law without signature' path (Article I §7, 10 days excl. Sundays from ~June 25 presentment) lands ~July 7-8, after the July 4 deadline
Small chance Trump reverses his public position for a symbolic July 4 signing win on a popular bipartisan bill

summary: H.R.6644 passed Congress June 23, enrolled June 25, on the President's desk. Trump publicly refused to sign on June 24 (Truth Social), demanding a national voter-ID law Congress cannot pass before July 4 — corroborated by four independent outlets. The Article I §7 automatic-enactment clock (10 days excluding Sundays from ~June 25 presentment) does not expire until ~July 7-8, after the deadline. Both YES paths are closed unless Trump reverses course and signs within days, contradicting his firm public stance. Residual probability reflects the small chance of a symbolic July 4 reversal.
reasoning:
**Final forecast: 4.5% (NO)**

H.R.6644 is very unlikely to become law before July 4, 2026. Both routes to enactment are blocked before the deadline: Trump has publicly refused to sign it (demanding voter-ID legislation Congress can't deliver in days), and the constitutional 10-day automatic-enactment clock from the ~June 25 presentment doesn't expire until roughly July 7-8 — after the deadline. The residual ~4.5% accounts for the small chance Trump reverses his stance for a symbolic Independence Day signing.
